lines.target: Outside the art gallery: — I liked that exhibition very much,
lines.base: A la salida de una galeria de arte: — Me ha gustado mucho esta exposicion,

lines.target: especially the modern painting of a man on a horse.
lines.base: especialmente la pintura moderna de un hombre a caballo.

lines.target: How do you know it was a man on a horse?
lines.base: ¿Cómo sabes que era un hombre a caballo ?

lines.target: Well, it was obvious, wasn't it?
In that case, it couldn't have been a modern painting.
lines.base: En ese caso no seria (no habría podido ser) una pintura moderna.

lines.target: A mother had just scolded her son and he started crying.
lines.base: Una madre acababa de reñir (había justo reñido) a su hijo y él comenzó a ilorar.

lines.target: At that moment, his father came in.
lines.base: En ese momento, su padre entró.

lines.target: What's the matter with you ? he asked.
The child turned his back and said nothing.
lines.base: El niño le dió la espalda (volvió su espalda) y [no] dijo nada.

lines.target: Come on!, said his father, tell Daddy.
lines.base: ¡Vamos! dijo su padre, dí [selo] a papá.

lines.target: The son turned round: — If you must know, I've just had an argument with your wife!
lines.base: El hijo se volvió: — Si quieres (si tienes que) saberlo, ¡acabo de tener una discusión con tu mujer!

lines.target: Employer. — We're looking for someone who is used to ordering men.
lines.base: Patrono. — Buscamos a alguien acostumbrado a mandar a los hombres.

lines.target: Man. — In that case, you want my wife.
lines.base: Hombre. — En ese caso, necesita usted a mi mujer.

lines.target: Do not forget to learn a few irregular verbs from time to time.
lines.base: No se olvide de aprender algunos verbos irregulares de vez en cuando.

gap_candidates.target: Outside the art gallery: — I liked that exhibition very much,
Well, it was obvious, wasn't it?
In that case, it couldn't have been a modern painting.
A mother had just scolded her son and he started crying.
At that moment, his father came in. (1)
What's the matter with you ? he asked.
The child turned his back and said nothing.
Come on!, said his father, tell Daddy. (2)
The son turned round: — If you must know, I've just had an argument with your wife!
